title: How can a user black-box a module when using the smt2 backend?
body:
I'm working with Symbiyosys, but I think this question is really about Yosys itself. If I want to work with a design that contains memories, I might not actually care about the exact behaviour of the memories - maybe I'm just interested in control signals for surrounding blocks.

The "obvious" thing to do would be to black-box the RTL module that represents the memory. Symbiyosys doesn't allow this, running a `hierarchy -simcheck` at the end of the script. It turns out that this is for a good reason! If you comment that check out, it seems that the SMT backend spits out something involving the black-boxed module that the solver doesn't like (I see something like `boolector: <stdin>:41:29: invalid sort '|$paramod/prim_rom_adv/Width=40/Depth=4096/MemInitFile=1'0_s|'.`).

Is there a proper way to black-box a module within the design?
